task_description: I1: Decouple Inline HTML from Python — Move ThemeMixin._render_theme_switcher() inline HTML/SVG/CSS from Python f-strings into Django templates. The mixin should render the same template as the tag version, not duplicate the HTML. Also move components.py HTML rendering to templates.

checklist.action: checkout fresh branch task/I1-decouple-inline-html from main
current structure and patterns — read mixins.py, components.py, theme_tags.py, and existing templates
code smells and issues — identify all inline HTML in Python
write analysis to .pipeline-state/task-I1-decouple-inline-html-analysis.md so later stages can read it
apply refactoring per analysis plan — move inline HTML from mixins.py and components.py to Django templates
preserve ALL existing behavior — no functional changes
write list of all changed files to .pipeline-state/task-I1-decouple-inline-html-changes.txt
run full test suite (read-only, no source modifications)
cross-ref analysis plan vs refactoring — flag unimplemented changes
read .pipeline-state/task-I1-decouple-inline-html-analysis.md to understand what was refactored
add/update docstrings for modified public APIs
update existing docs that reference moved/renamed code
verify no broken internal links or orphaned docs
read .pipeline-state/task-I1-decouple-inline-html-changes.txt for list of files modified
git remote -v — verify github remote exists
git add -u to stage ALL modified tracked files
git add each file from changes.txt to catch new files

9.name: Code Review
9.subagent_prompt: You are reviewing PR #{pr_number} for a refactor. Project: {project_path}. PR targets {pr_target_branch}. Step 0: Read .pipeline-state/*-analysis.md for the original analysis. Step 1: Run git diff {pr_target_branch}...HEAD, read changed files. Check docs/PULL_REQUEST_CHECKLIST.md if exists. Step 2: Cross-ref analysis vs implementation — verify all planned changes were made. CRITICAL: Verify behavior is preserved — refactor must NOT change functionality. Step 3: Review for correctness, security, testing, quality, docs, perf. Flag auto-reject triggers. Step 4: Run project test suite, report pass/fail. Step 5: Decide APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/COMMENT. Step 6 MANDATORY: Post review to GitHub with gh pr review {pr_number} --comment --body '<formatted review>'. If REQUEST_CHANGES use --request-changes. Save to pr/feedback/ if dir exists. Step 7: Output verdict.

checklist.action: load project PR checklist if exists
cross-ref analysis vs implementation — all planned changes made
verify behavior preserved — refactor must NOT change functionality
review: correctness, security, testing, quality, docs, perf
run full test suite, report pass/fail
post review to GitHub: gh pr review --comment with formatted checklist and findings
save to pr/feedback/ if directory exists

10.name: Review Verdict
10.note: Verdict is determined by the Code Review subagent above. If APPROVE, proceed to Merge. If REQUEST_CHANGES, fix issues, re-commit, re-push, re-run Code Review.

checklist.action: if REQUEST_CHANGES: fix issues, re-commit, re-push, re-run stage 9

11.name: Merge PR
11.subagent_prompt: Merge PR #{pr_number} in {project_path}. Run: gh pr merge {pr_number} --squash --delete-branch. Do NOT run gh pr review --approve (GitHub blocks self-approval). Output PR_MERGED or MERGE_FAILED.

12.name: Retrospective
12.subagent_prompt: Write retrospective for refactor: {task_description}. Project: {project_path}. PR #{pr_number}. Run git log {pr_target_branch}..HEAD. Rate quality 1-5, what went well, lessons learned, improvements. MANDATORY: Post retro as gh pr comment {pr_number} --body '<formatted retro with quality, lessons, improvements>'. MANDATORY: Append to .pipeline-log.md (create if needed, ensure in .gitignore). Output RETRO_COMPLETE.

checklist.action: post retrospective to GitHub PR: gh pr comment
